children.heading: Short-term supported housing
children.label: 1 Short-term supported housing
children.text: Purchasing, leasing, renovating, repairing, and converting facilities to provide short-term shelter and services.

children.heading: Short-term housing payments assistance
children.label: 2 Short-term housing payments assistance
children.text: Providing rent assistance payments for short-term supported housing and rent, mortgage, and utilities payments to prevent homelessness of the tenant or mortgagor of a dwelling.

children.heading: Supportive services
children.label: 3 Supportive services
children.text: Providing supportive services, to eligible persons assisted under paragraphs (1) and (2), including health, mental health, assessment, permanent housing placement, drug and alcohol abuse treatment and counseling, day care, and nutritional services (except that health services under this paragraph may only be provided to individuals with acquired immunodeficiency syndrome or related diseases), and providing technical assistance to eligible persons to provide assistance in gaining access to benefits and services for homeless individuals provided by the Federal Government and State and local governments.

children.heading: Operation
children.label: 4 Operation
children.text: Providing for the operation of short-term supported housing provided under this section, including the costs of security, operation insurance, utilities, furnishings, equipment, supplies, and other incidental costs.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 5 Administration
children.text: Providing staff to carry out the program under this section (subject to the provisions of ).section 12905(g) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of assistance involving substantial rehabilitation or acquisition of the building, for a period of not less than 10 years; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of assistance under paragraph (1), (3), or (4) of subsection (a), for a period of not less than 3 years.

children.label: i
children.text: the structure is no longer needed to provide short-term supported housing or assistance or the continued operation of the structure for such purposes is no longer feasible; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the structure will be used to benefit individuals or families whose incomes do not exceed 80 percent of the median income for the area, as determined by the Secretary, with adjustments for smaller and larger families, except that the Secretary may establish income ceilings higher or lower than 80 percent of the median income for the area if the Secretary finds that such variations are necessary because of prevailing levels of construction costs or unusually high or low family incomes.

children.heading: Residency and location limitations on short-term supported housing
children.label: 2 Residency and location limitations on short-term supported housing

children.heading: Residency
children.label: A Residency
children.text: A short-term supported housing facility assisted with amounts from a grant under this section may not provide shelter or housing at any single time for more than 50 families or individuals.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: B Waiver
children.text: The Secretary may, as the Secretary determines appropriate, waive the limitation under subparagraph (A) for any program or short-term supported housing facility.

children.heading: Supported housing assistance
children.label: A Supported housing assistance
children.text: A program assisted under this section may not provide residence in a short-term housing facility assisted under this section to any individual for a sum of more than 60 days during any 6-month period.

children.heading: Housing payments assistance
children.label: B Housing payments assistance
children.text: A program assisted under this section may not provide assistance for rent, mortgage, or utilities payments to any individual for rent, mortgage, or utilities costs accruing over a period of more than 21 weeks of any 52-week period.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: C Waiver
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraphs (A) and (B), the Secretary may waive the applicability of the requirements under such subparagraphs with respect to any individual for which the project sponsor has made a good faith effort to acquire permanent housing (in accordance with paragraph (4)) and has been unable to do so.

children.heading: Placement
children.label: 4 Placement
children.text: A program assisted under this section shall provide for any individual who has remained in short-term supported housing assisted under the demonstration program, to the maximum extent practicable, the opportunity for placement in permanent housing or an environment appropriate to the health and social needs of the individual.

children.heading: Presumption for independent living
children.label: 5 Presumption for independent living
children.text: In providing assistance under this section in any case in which the residence of an individual is appropriate to the needs of the individual, a program assisted under this section shall, when reasonable, provide for assistance in a manner appropriate to maintain the individual in such residence.

children.heading: Case management services
children.label: 6 Case management services
children.text: A program assisted under this section shall provide each individual assisted under the program with an opportunity, if eligible, to receive case management services available from the appropriate social service agencies.
